title: Change Processing Message from 'Your Data' to 'Meta-Data'
story: As a DataTools Pro user running the Metrics Analyst, I want the processing message to say 'Processing meta-data' instead of 'Processing your data', so that I understand the tool only accesses metadata and not my actual report data.
description: A customer reported confusion when seeing 'Processing your data' — they believed DataTools Pro was downloading their actual report data, which is not the case. The system only processes metadata (dashboard structure, report names, widget types). Changing the message to 'Processing meta-data' removes the ambiguity and aligns with the product's privacy posture.
acceptance_criteria: Processing message reads 'Processing meta-data' instead of 'Processing your data'
Message is clear that no actual report data is being accessed
ux_changes: Changed the processing message from 'Processing your data' to 'Processing meta-data' to reduce customer confusion about data access scope.
implementation_notes: This change was prompted by a customer call where the user was concerned about data privacy.
comment_summary: Developer updated the message on staging.
